description_long: A cluster of eight red-capped mushrooms with white spots is shown, each with a smooth, rounded cap and a slender, cylindrical stem. The caps are a vibrant red with evenly distributed, large white circular spots, reminiscent of the classic Amanita muscaria appearance. The stems are pale grayish-white, straight to slightly curved, and taper gently toward the base. The mushrooms vary in size, with some noticeably taller and broader than others, suggesting a natural, non-uniform growth pattern. The surface of the caps appears smooth and slightly matte, with no visible gills or underside details. The group is arranged closely together, as if sprouting from a shared patch of ground, suitable for placement in parks, gardens, or decorative urban green spaces.
description: A group of eight red mushrooms with white spots and pale stems, featuring smooth, rounded caps and varying heights. The cluster has a natural, whimsical appearance ideal for urban parks or decorative settings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cluster with the tallest mushrooms in the center and smaller ones on the sides, highlighting the rounded caps and spot patterns.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the curvature and varying angles of the stems, with the caps' spots still prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the backs of the caps and the arrangement of the mushrooms, with the stems mostly hidden.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the lean and orientation of the stems, with the caps' spots visible from a different angle.
